## Get User Task List

Returns the full record for a user task list. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/user_task_lists/{user_task_list_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User task list gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the user task list.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. ## Get Tasks For User Task List

`<asana:get-user-task-lists-tasks-by-user-task-list-gid>`

Returns the compact list of tasks in a userâs My Tasks list.

Access control is enforced for this endpoint, as with all Asana API endpoint. A userâs private tasks
are filtered out if the API-authenticated user does not have access to them. Both complete and
incomplete tasks are returned by default unless they are filtered out (for example, setting
`completed_since=now` returns only incomplete tasks, which is the default view for 'My Tasks' in
Asana.) This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /user_task_lists/{user_task_list_gid}/tasks
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User task list gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the user task list.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Completed since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return tasks that are either incomplete or that have been completed since this time. Accepts a date-time string or the keyword <strong>now</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. - ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND

## Get Users

`<asana:get-users>`

Returns the user records for all users in all workspaces and organizations accessible to the
authenticated user. Accepts an optional workspace ID parameter. Results are sorted by user ID. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>workspace</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The workspace or organization ID to filter users on.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get User

`<asana:get-users-by-user-gid>`

Returns the full user record for the single user with the provided ID. Results are sorted by user
ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{user_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string identifying a user. This can either be the string "me", an email, or the gid of a user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#configurations)

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Get Favorites For User

`<asana:get-users-favorites-by-user-gid>`

Returns all of a userâs favorites in the given workspace, of the given type. Results are given in
order (The same order as Asanaâs sidebar). This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/users/{user_gid}/favorites endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string identifying a user. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. ## Get Team Memberships For User

`<asana:get-users-team-memberships-by-user-gid>`

Returns the compact team membership records for the user. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /users/{user_gid}/team_memberships endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string identifying a user. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>workspace</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. ## Get Teams For User

`<asana:get-users-teams-by-user-gid>`

Returns the compact records for all teams to which the given user is assigned. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /users/{user_gid}/teams endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string identifying a user. This can either be the string "me", an email, or the gid of a user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>organization</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The workspace or organization to filter teams on.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get User Task List For User

`<asana:get-users-user-task-list-by-user-gid>`

Returns the full record for a user''s task list. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/users/{user_gid}/user_task_list endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string identifying a user. This can either be the string "me", an email, or the gid of a user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>workspace</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The workspace in which to get the user task list.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

`<asana:get-users-workspace-memberships-by-user-gid>`

Returns the compact workspace membership records for the user. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /users/{user_gid}/workspace_memberships endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string identifying a user. ## Get Webhooks

`<asana:get-webhooks>`

Get the compact representation of all webhooks your app has registered for the authenticated user in
the given workspace. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /webhooks endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>workspace</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The workspace to query for webhooks in.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>resource</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return webhooks for the given resource.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. ## Get Webhook

`<asana:get-webhooks-by-webhook-gid>`

/webhooks/{webhook_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webhook gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the webhook.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Workspace Membership

`<asana:get-workspace-memberships-by-workspace-membership-gid>`

Returns the complete workspace record for a single workspace membership. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /workspace_memberships/{workspace_membership_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace membership gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Workspace membership gid</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- ASANA:NOT_FOUND

## Get Workspaces

`<asana:get-workspaces>`

Returns the compact records for all workspaces visible to the authorized user. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /workspaces endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-by-workspace-gid>`

Returns the full workspace record for a single workspace. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /workspaces/{workspace_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get Custom Fields For Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-custom-fields-by-workspace-gid>`

Returns a list of the compact representation of all of the custom fields in a workspace. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /workspaces/{workspace_gid}/custom_fields endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. ### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Projects For Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-projects-by-workspace-gid>`

Returns the compact project records for all projects in the workspace.

**NOTE:** This endpoint might time out for large domains. It is better to use the
`/teams/{team_gid}/projects` endpoint.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /workspaces/{workspace_gid}/projects endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>archived</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return projects whose <code>archived</code> field takes on the value of this parameter.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Tags For Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-tags-by-workspace-gid>`

Returns the compact tag records for some filtered set of tags. Use one or more of the parameters
provided to filter the tags returned. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/workspaces/{workspace_gid}/tags endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. ## Search Tasks For Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-tasks-search-by-workspace-gid>`

To mirror the functionality of the Asana web appâs advanced search feature, the Asana API has a task
search endpoint that allows you to build complex filters to find and retrieve the exact data you
need. Premium access such as the Asana web productâs advanced search feature is only available to
premium Asana users. A user is premium if any of the following is true:

- The workspace in which the search is being performed is a premium workspace
- The user is a member of a premium team inside the workspace, even if a user is only a member of a
  premium team inside a non-premium workspace, the search will allow them to find data anywhere in
  the workspace, not just inside the premium team.

Making a search request using credentials of a non-premium user will result in a
`402 Payment Required` error.

Pagination Search results are not stable; repeating the same query multiple times may return the
data in a different order, even if the data does not change. Because of this, the traditional
\[pagination\]([https://developers.asana.com/docs/overview](https://developers.asana.com/docs/overview))
available elsewhere in the Asana API is not available here.

However, you can paginate manually by sorting the search results by their creation time and then
modifying each subsequent query to exclude data you have already seen. Page sizes are limited to a
maximum of 100 items, and can be specified by the `limit` query parameter.

Eventual consistency Changes in Asana (regardless of whether theyâre made though the web product or
the API) are forwarded to our search infrastructure to be indexed. This process can take between 10
and 60 seconds to complete under normal operation, and longer during some production incidents.

Making a change to a task that would alter its presence in a particular search query will not be
reflected immediately. This is also true of the advanced search feature in the web product.

Rate limits - You might receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response if you hit any of our \[rate
limits\]([https://developers.asana.com/docs/overview](https://developers.asana.com/docs/overview)).

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created on before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created by any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Due on after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Tags all</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of tag IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Liked by not</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Modified on</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string or <code>null</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Is blocked</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter to tasks with incomplete dependencies</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Liked by any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Modified on after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Is blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter to incomplete tasks with dependents</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start on</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string or <code>null</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Teams any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of team IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty' output. Provides the response in a 'pretty' format. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Tags any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of tag IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Portfolios any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of portfolio IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Followers not</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Due at after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 datetime string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sort ascending</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Projects not</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of project IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Is subtask</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter to subtasks</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Performs full-text search on both task name and description</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Tags not</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of tag IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created at before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 datetime string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start on after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Completed on</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string or <code>null</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created at after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 datetime string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sections not</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of section or column IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>completed</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter to completed tasks</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Modified at after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 datetime string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start on before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Followers any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Modified at before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 datetime string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sort by</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DUE_DATE</p></li><li><p>CREATED_AT</p></li><li><p>COMPLETED_AT</p></li><li><p>LIKES</p></li><li><p>MODIFIED_AT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>One of <code>due_date</code>, <code>created_at</code>, <code>completed_at</code>, <code>likes</code>, or <code>modified_at</code>, defaults to <code>modified_at</code></p></td><td><p>MODIFIED_AT</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects in order to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The id of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Assigned by any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Assigned by not</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Projects all</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of project IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Completed on after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Projects any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of project IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sections all</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of section or column IDs</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Completed at after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 datetime string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Has attachment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter to tasks with attachments</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Completed on before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created by not</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Commented on by any</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of user identifiers</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Due on before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ISO 8601 date string</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource subtype</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DEFAULT_TASK</p></li><li><p>MILESTONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by the taskâs resource_subtype</p></td><td><p>MILESTONE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable on which the operationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that will be evaluated against the operationâs output and the outcome of that expression will be stored in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Typeahead For Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-typeahead-by-workspace-gid>`

Retrieves objects in the workspace based on an auto-completion or typeahead search algorithm. This
feature is meant to provide results quickly, so do not rely on this API to provide extremely
accurate search results. The result set is limited to a single page of results with a maximum size,
so you wonât be able to fetch large numbers of results.

he typeahead search API provides search for objects from a single workspace. This endpoint should be
used to query for objects when creating an auto-completion/typeahead search feature. This API is
meant to provide results quickly and should not be relied upon for accurate or exhaustive search
results. The results sets are limited in size and cannot be paginated. Queries return a compact
representation of each object which is typically the gid and name fields.

For a specific set of fields or all of the fields, use field selectors to manipulate what data is
included in a response. Resources with type `user` are returned in order of most contacted to least
contacted. This is determined by task assignments, adding the user to projects, and adding the user
as a follower to tasks, messages, and so forth.

Resources with type `project` are returned in order of recency. This is determined when the user
visits the project, is added to the project, and completes tasks in the project. Resources with type
`task` are returned with priority placed on tasks the user is following, but no guarantee on the
order of those tasks. Leaving the `query` string empty or omitted will give you results, by
following the resource ordering as noted previously.

This could be used to list users or projects that are relevant for the requesting userâs api token.
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /workspaces/{workspace_gid}/typeahead endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of results to return. The default is 20 if this parameter is omitted, with a minimum of 1 and a maximum of 100. If there are fewer results found than requested, all will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CUSTOM_FIELD</p></li><li><p>PORTFOLIO</p></li><li><p>PROJECT</p></li><li><p>TAG</p></li><li><p>TASK</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of values the typeahead should return. You can choose from one of the following: <code>custom_field</code>, <code>project</code>, <code>portfolio</code>, <code>tag</code>, <code>task</code>, and <code>user</code>. Note that unlike in the names of endpoint.s, the types listed here are in singular form (, for example <code>task</code>). Using multiple types is not yet supported.</p></td><td><p>USER</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>query</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The string to use to search for relevant objects. If an empty string is passed in, the API will currently return an empty result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CUSTOM_FIELD</p></li><li><p>PORTFOLIO</p></li><li><p>PROJECT</p></li><li><p>TAG</p></li><li><p>TASK</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><strong>Deprecated: new integrations should prefer the resource_type field.</strong></p></td><td><p>USER</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Users For Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-users-by-workspace-gid>`

Returns the user records for all users in the specified workspace or organization. Results are
sorted alphabetically by user names. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/workspaces/{workspace_gid}/users endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return.
