Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get Workspace Memberships For Workspace

`<asana:get-workspaces-workspace-memberships-by-workspace-gid>`

Returns the compact workspace membership records for the workspace. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /workspaces/{workspace_gid}/workspace_memberships endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>user</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A string identifying a user. This can either be the string "me", an email, or the gid of a user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Unauthorize

`<asana:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that itâs impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the resource owner which access should be invalidated</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

## Update Custom Field

`<asana:update-custom-fields-by-custom-field-gid>`

A specific, existing custom field can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that custom
field. Only the fields provided in the `data` block are updated; any unspecified fields remain
unchanged when using this method. It is best to specify only those fields you want to change, or you
may overwrite changes made by another user since you last retrieved the custom field.

A custom fieldâs `type` cannot be updated. An enum custom fieldâs `enum_options` cannot be updated
with this endpoint. Instead see 'Work With Enum Options' for information on how to update
`enum_options`.

Locked custom fields can only be updated by the user who locked the field. Returns the complete
updated custom field record. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/custom_fields/{custom_field_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom field gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the custom field.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Fields Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT

## Update Enum Option

`<asana:update-enum-options-by-enum-option-gid>`

Updates an existing enum option. Enum custom fields require at least one enabled enum option. Locked
custom fields can only be updated by the user who locked the field. Returns the full record of the
updated enum option. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /enum_options/{enum_option_gid}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enum option gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the enum option.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. ## Update Portfolio

`<asana:update-portfolios-by-portfolio-gid>`

An existing portfolio can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that portfolio. Only the
fields provided in the `data` block will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.
Returns the complete updated portfolio record. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/portfolios/{portfolio_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Portfolio gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the portfolio.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. ## Update Project

`<asana:update-projects-by-project-gid>`

A specific, existing project can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that project.
Only the fields provided in the `data` block are updated; any unspecified fields remain unchanged.
When using this method, it is best to specify only those fields to change, or you may overwrite
changes made by another user since you last retrieved the task.

Returns the complete updated project record. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/projects/{project_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Project gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the project.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Projects Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update Section

`<asana:update-sections-by-section-gid>`

A specific, existing section can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that project.
Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain
unchanged. (note that at this time, the only field that can be updated is the `name` field.) When
using this method, it is best to specify only those fields you wish to change, or else you may
overwrite changes made by another user since you last retrieved the task. Returns the complete
updated section record. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /sections/{section_gid}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Section gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The globally unique identifier for the section.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sections Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update Story

`<asana:update-stories-by-story-gid>`

Updates the story and returns the full record for the updated story. Only comment stories can have
their text updated, and only comment stories and attachment stories can be pinned. Only one of
`text` and `html_text` can be specified. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/stories/{story_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Story gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the story.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stories Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update Tag

`<asana:update-tags-by-tag-gid>`

Updates the properties of a tag. Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be updated; any
unspecified fields will remain unchanged. When using this method, it is best to specify only those
fields you wish to change, or else you may overwrite changes made by another user since you last
retrieved the tag. Returns the complete updated tag record. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request
to the /tags/{tag_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Tag gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the tag.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Results per page. The number of objects to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Offset token. An offset to the next page returned by the API. A pagination request will return an offset token, which can be used as an input parameter to the next request. If an offset is not passed in, the API will return the first page of results. 'Note: You can only pass in an offset that was returned to you via a previously paginated request.'</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR

## Update Task

`<asana:update-tasks-by-task-gid>`

A specific, existing task can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that task. Only the
fields provided in the `data` block will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.
When using this method, it is best to specify only those fields you wish to change, or else you may
overwrite changes made by another user since you last retrieved the task. Returns the complete
updated task record. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /tasks/{task_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Task gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The task to operate on.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Tasks Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR
- ASANA:CONNECTIVITY
- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- ASANA:NOT_FOUND
- ASANA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- ASANA:SERVER_ERROR
- ASANA:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- ASANA:TIMEOUT
- ASANA:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- ASANA:UNAUTHORIZED
- ASANA:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update Workspace

`<asana:update-workspaces-by-workspace-gid>`

A specific, existing workspace can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that workspace.
Only the fields provided in the data block will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain
unchanged. Currently the only field that can be modified for a workspace is its name. Returns the
complete, updated workspace record. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/workspaces/{workspace_gid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace gid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Globally unique identifier for the workspace or organization.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines fields to return. Some requests return <strong>compact</strong> representations of objects to conserve resources and complete the request more efficiently. Other times requests return more information than you may need. This option allows you to list the exact set of fields that the API should be sure to return for the objects. The field names should be provided as paths, described below. The ID of included objects will always be returned, regardless of the field options.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspaces Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- ASANA:BAD_REQUEST
- ASANA:CLIENT_ERROR

- ASANA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- ASANA:NOT_FOUND

## Sources

## On Updated Task Trigger

`<asana:on-updated-task-trigger>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Modified since</p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return tasks that have been modified since the given time.</p><p>NOTE: A task is considered 'modified' if any of its properties change, or associations between it and other objects are modified (, for example a task being added to a project). A task is not considered modified just because another object it is associated with (, for example a subtask) is modified. Actions that count as modifying the task include assigning, renaming, completing, and adding stories.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Assignee</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The assignee to filter tasks on. <strong>Note: If you specify <code>assignee</code>, you must also specify the <code>workspace</code> to filter on.</strong></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workspace</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The workspace to filter tasks on. <strong>Note: If you specify <code>workspace</code>, you must also specify the <code>assignee</code> to filter on.</strong></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Project</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The project to filter tasks on.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Section</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The section to filter tasks on. <strong>Note: Currently, this is only supported in board views.</strong></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Opt pretty</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Provides 'pretty'Âoutput. Provides the response in a 'pretty'Âformat. In the case of JSON this means doing proper line breaking and indentation to make it readable. This will take extra time and increase the response size so it is advisable only to use this during debugging.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure if repeatable streams should be used and their behavior</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tls

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">Crl File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Http Response Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Status Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reason Phrase</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory that will be allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines which strategy is used to identify the messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if useSecureHash is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of instances to initially keep in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Asana Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Asana (Asana Connector) synchronizes data and automates business processes
between Asana and third-party applications, either on-premises or in the cloud.

Asana is a SaaS project management tool that enhances team collaboration. Employees can easily
manage projects and tasks through one integrated collaborative project management interface when
using Asana.

For compatibility information and fixed issues, see the Asana Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Asana APIs
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the target resource
- Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

Automate powerful business processes by integrating Asana with other IT tools and systems in your
enterprise such as messaging, calendar, and issue triaging systems. Possible uses include:

- Improve cross-team communication by automating a workflow where users get notified (via a
  messaging system such as Slack) anytime a task or comment is created in Asana.
- Enhance sales collaboration by automating the lead tracking and nurturing process where any time a
  new lead or opportunity is created in your CRM system (such as Salesforce), a task is created and
  assigned to a user via your Asana dashboard for triaging and tracking.
- Help teams stay organized by integrating the teamâs calendar with an Asana workspace so that
  anytime a new team event is created, it gets posted to the Asana workspace.

## Authentication Types

Asana Connector connections use the following authentication methods:

- OAuth 2.0
- Unsecure connection provider

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Asana Reference](asana-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
